message:
refactor(pattern_trigger): leftmost-first dedup across exact + case-insensitive automata

TriggerScanner previously ran two independent find_iter passes over
the same input: one through `self.automaton` (exact patterns) and one
through `self.automaton_ci` (case-insensitive patterns). Each pass
emitted its matches independently, and both scan_counts() and
scan_locate() then merged the results — scan_counts simply bumped
per-category counts for every match, and scan_locate pushed every
match into a Vec and sorted by offset at the end.

The problem: an input like `"ERROR: ..."` would hit `ERROR` in the
exact automaton AND `error` (case-insensitive) in the nocase
automaton, producing TWO matches at offset 0 with length 5. Downstream
consumers that expect one match per overlapping region (category
routing, trigger-firing deduplication, severity classification)
silently counted one event as two.

## Fix: extract leftmost-first dedup into `for_each_leftmost_match`

New private helper that:

  1. Obtains peekable iterators over both automata's find_iter
     streams (nocase optional — build is lazy).
  2. Maintains a `next_allowed_offset` cursor that starts at 0.
  3. Advances each iterator past any match whose `offset <
     next_allowed_offset`.
  4. Picks the frontier match: earliest-offset wins; ties resolved
     by pattern_index (stable, deterministic, matches insertion
     order).
  5. Invokes the caller's FnMut(TriggerMatch) on the selected match.
  6. Sets `next_allowed_offset = matched.offset + matched.length`
     so subsequent matches that start inside the current match are
     silently dropped.

Both scan_counts() and scan_locate() now funnel through this helper:

    pub fn scan_counts(...) -> TriggerScanResult {
        let mut result = ...;
        self.for_each_leftmost_match(input, |matched| {
            *result.counts.entry(matched.category).or_insert(0) += 1;
            result.total_matches += 1;
        });
        result
    }

    pub fn scan_locate(...) -> Vec<TriggerMatch> {
        let mut matches = Vec::new();
        self.for_each_leftmost_match(input, |matched| matches.push(matched));
        matches
    }

scan_locate's explicit `matches.sort_by_key(|m| m.offset)` post-pass
is deleted because the helper emits matches in strictly-increasing
offset order by construction — the peek/cursor loop picks the
earliest frontier match each iteration.

Two small private helpers (exact_trigger_match, nocase_trigger_match)
pull the TriggerMatch construction out of the hot loop for
readability; they're inlined into the iterator .map() closures.

## Regression test

`overlapping_exact_and_case_insensitive_patterns_follow_leftmost_first_once`:
register two patterns whose literal spans overlap — an exact "ERROR"
(Custom category) and a case-insensitive "error" (Error category).
Scan `"ERROR: duplicated?"`. Before the dedup, scan_counts returned
total_matches=2 (one Custom, one Error). After, total_matches=1 and
the winning pattern is the exact "ERROR" at Custom category
(insertion-order tiebreak — the exact pattern was registered first).

## Behavior impact

Consumers that expected the OLD counting (one call per automaton
hit) will see fewer events. Grep across the workspace shows
scan_counts + scan_locate are consumed only by the trigger
evaluator, which already dedupes at a higher layer — so this is a
strict improvement, not a breaking change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
